message:
perf(indexer): thread total_conversations into rebuild state without recounting

Follow-up to facd9df2 which already eliminated two redundant
`count_total_conversations_exact(&storage)` calls on the salvage +
rebuild paths. `rebuild_tantivy_from_db` still made two more of the
same call:

  1. `lexical_rebuild_db_state(&storage, db_path)` — computes a
     fresh `LexicalRebuildDbState`, which the original implementation
     always materialised by recounting.
  2. If the Tantivy index was corrupt and had to be recreated, a
     second `lexical_rebuild_db_state(&storage, db_path)` call built
     a second `LexicalRebuildState::new(...)` with the same data.

Split `lexical_rebuild_db_state` into two functions:

  * `lexical_rebuild_db_state_with_total_conversations(storage,
    db_path, total_conversations)` — the pure shaping helper. No
    DB scan.
  * `lexical_rebuild_db_state(storage, db_path)` — the convenience
    wrapper that still performs the count for callers outside the
    rebuild loop.

Use the precomputed `total_conversations` (which the caller already
has) in `rebuild_tantivy_from_db`, and on the corrupt-index recovery
branch reuse `db_state.clone()` instead of rebuilding the state
from scratch. `LexicalRebuildDbState` is a thin struct (path,
schema hash, counts) so the clone is effectively free.

Net effect on the streamed rebuild hot path: two fewer full
conversation-count scans. Consistent with the phase-breakdown
in the benchmarking doc — prepare leg is the dominant remaining
fixed cost.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
